zotero.abstractNote: Semi-private learning is a framework where a learner has access to a small amount of private data and a large amount of public data. While this setting is well-motivated, existing algorithms for semi-private learning often fail to fully leverage the public data, leading to suboptimal performance. In this work, we propose a new approach to semi-private learning that effectively utilizes public data to improve the privacy-utility trade-off. Our method is based on a novel combination of private empirical risk minimization and public data-driven regularization. We provide theoretical guarantees for our approach and demonstrate its effectiveness through extensive experiments on various datasets.
zotero.conferenceName: TPDP 2023 - Theory and Practice of Differential Privacy
zotero.proceedingsTitle: TPDP 2023 - Theory and Practice of Differential Privacy
